title: Thanks to Trump, Russia’s Own ‘Pivot to Asia’ Is Bearing Fruit
gist: A teaser excerpt arguing that the U.S.-Israel war against Iran and the resulting global energy crisis turned an otherwise routine ASEAN-Russia summit in Kazan into a geostrategically significant event, advancing Russia's pivot to Asia at Washington's expense.

insights.claim: ASEAN leaders including Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. visited Kazan, Russia, and signed a joint statement with Putin promoting a 'just multipolar world' guided by international law and the UN Charter.
insights.speaker: CNAS
insights.so_what: Southeast Asian states publicly aligning with a Russian multipolarity framing signals drift away from a U.S.-centric order in the region.
insights.quote: just multipolar world as guided by international law and the principles of the UN Charter

insights.claim: The global energy crisis caused by the U.S.-Israel war against Iran is what made the 2026 Russia-ASEAN summit geostrategically significant, whereas under normal circumstances it would have gone unnoticed.
insights.speaker: CNAS
insights.so_what: Links Middle East conflict directly to Russia's strengthened Asian energy diplomacy; an energy-supply shock is reshaping bloc alignments.
insights.quote: within the context of the global energy crisis caused by the U.S.-Israel war against Iran, the Russia-ASEAN summit this time around was geostrategically significant

themes.theme: U.S. strategic exposure in Southeast Asia

insights.claim: To hold its strategic position in Southeast Asia, Washington must do more than compete with China; it must also avoid handing Russia opportunities it could not have created on its own.
insights.speaker: CNAS
insights.so_what: Reframes U.S. Asia strategy as a two-front problem; self-inflicted openings (e.g., the Iran war's energy fallout) benefit Russia, not just China.
insights.quote: It will also need to avoid handing Russia opportunities that it could not have created on its own.
insights.locator: If Washington wants to maintain paragraph
